cautions: Do NOT play more than one DM — CNS warmup fades fast, extra DMs waste the effect
Do NOT chase DM score — you are activating the nervous system, not proving yourself
Do NOT extend beyond 15 min — you will fatigue before ranked starts
Do NOT check your phone between RAMP end and ranked queue

steps.title: RAMP — Raise
steps.detail: Valorant range. Loose shots at medium bots. Head height only. No pressure — wake up the hands.

steps.title: RAMP — Activate + Mobilize
steps.detail: Ghost DM or range. Slow tracking shots, micro-corrections. Wide mouse swipes across the full pad to warm up arm range of motion.

steps.title: Potentiate DM — your drill cue only
steps.detail: DM with your chosen weapon. Apply only your ONE drill cue (shown above). Do not try to win. Stop while still feeling sharp — not exhausted.

steps.title: Queue ranked immediately
steps.detail: No delay. CNS activation window closes fast. Three deep breaths, recall your ONE cue, queue.

cautions: 15 min is warmup territory only — switch to Ranked mode if you only have 15 min
Do NOT jump in without a specific cue in mind — vague practice builds vague habits

steps.title: RAMP — Raise + Activate
steps.detail: Range: loose shots → tracking. Get hands warm. Do not rush.

steps.title: Drill — high reps
steps.detail: Your recommended drill (shown above). Apply the cue with full focus. Count reps. Do not switch drills mid-session.

cautions: Do NOT switch drills mid-session — stick to ONE mechanic so it has time to transfer
Do NOT use a rifle for DM if drilling crosshair placement — Ghost or Sheriff only
Do NOT skip the review — 5 honest minutes beats 30 mindless ones

steps.title: RAMP — full (all 4 phases)
steps.detail: Range: loose bots → Ghost tracking → wide pad swipes → brief potentiate DM. Finish the full RAMP. Do not rush.

steps.title: Drill A — deliberate reps
steps.detail: Your primary drill (shown above). Slow, intentional reps — say the cue out loud before each one. Errors are good: they are the learning signal.

steps.title: DM — same cue in live chaos
steps.detail: Your chosen weapon. Carry only the drill cue. Ignore your score. Every kill should use the drill mechanic — if you win via a flick, that rep does not count.

steps.title: Review — 3–5 deaths, honest
steps.detail: Was each death mechanical (missed timing, bad stop) or tactical (positioning, info)? Apply 5-Why to ONE death. Write the root cause in the Tracker.

steps.title: Pre-ranked reset
steps.detail: ONE cue out loud or written. Three slow exhales. Visualize one round going correctly. Queue.

cautions: Training day = no ranked. Pink mode (analytical) does not switch cleanly to Green mode — do not play ranked after
Do NOT practice more than 2 things — one mechanic drilled properly beats five touched once

steps.title: RAMP — all 4 phases
steps.detail: All phases at a relaxed pace. Today you are not rushing to queue.

steps.title: Drill A — deliberate reps
steps.detail: Your primary drill (shown above). High reps. Say the cue out loud. The mistake IS the rep — fail, reset, repeat.

steps.title: DM transfer — same mechanic
steps.detail: Ghost or Guardian DM. Carry only the drill cue. Count kills that used the mechanic vs. kills that did not. Score is irrelevant.

steps.title: Reflection + log
steps.detail: Open Tracker. Write: 1 thing that worked, 1 thing to fix, cue for next session. Then 5 min idle (eyes closed, no phone) — consolidation.

cautions: Stop warmup at 15 min no matter how good you feel — everything after is training, not warmup
Do NOT run more than 2 drills — cognitive overload degrades transfer to ranked
Do NOT tilt after the first loss — trust the process for a minimum 3-game sample

steps.title: RAMP — all 4 phases
steps.detail: Raise, Activate, Mobilize, Potentiate — all four, full quality.

steps.title: Drill A — primary mechanic
steps.detail: Your primary drill (shown above). Slow, deliberate reps. Say the cue before each rep. Build the groove before building speed.

steps.title: Drill B — secondary mechanic
steps.detail: Your secondary drill (shown above). Only start B if A felt solid — otherwise continue A. Do not jump categories.

steps.title: Potentiate DM — chosen weapon
steps.detail: Transfer both mechanics into live DM. One primary cue only. Not playing to win — playing to carry the drill mechanic into chaos.

steps.title: Review — kills + deaths
steps.detail: Were kills from the drill mechanic or from flicking? Were deaths mechanical or tactical? Write one finding in the Tracker.

steps.title: Pre-ranked reset
steps.detail: ONE cue from Drill A. Three exhales. Visualize one round — the drill mechanic executed correctly. Queue.

cautions: No ranked today — honor the training day commitment
Do NOT do more than 2 drills — depth beats breadth every time

steps.title: RAMP — all 4 phases
steps.detail: All phases, relaxed pace. No rush today.

steps.title: Drill A — deep reps
steps.detail: Your primary drill. High reps. Wrong reps do not count — redo them. The error IS the signal.

steps.title: DM transfer — Drill A in chaos
steps.detail: Ghost or Guardian DM. Only the drill cue matters. Score is irrelevant — count drill mechanic kills vs. non-mechanic kills.

steps.title: Drill B — secondary
steps.detail: Your secondary drill. Stay in the same category as Drill A where possible. Do not jump from aim to mental in one session.

steps.title: Log + idle rest
steps.detail: Open Tracker: write 1 mistake, 1 success, cue for next session. Then 5 min eyes closed, no phone — free consolidation.

cautions: Still stop warmup at 15 min even with 60 min — over-warming burns CNS capacity before ranked
Do NOT VOD review more than 5 min before ranked — Pink mode does not switch off cleanly
Do NOT run 3+ drills — go deep on two

steps.title: RAMP — full, all 4 phases
steps.detail: All phases with full quality. Finish feeling sharp and activated, not fatigued.

steps.title: Drill A — deep focus
steps.detail: Your primary drill (shown above). Maximum deliberate attention. If you hit the completion condition, rest 60 seconds, then continue.

steps.title: DM transfer — Drill A
steps.detail: Ghost, Guardian, or Sheriff. ONE cue only. Log mechanical errors for the review step. No score pressure.

steps.title: Drill B or VOD
steps.detail: If sharp: run Drill B (shown above). If tired: 8 min VOD — one clip, one death, one insight only. Do not do both.

steps.title: Final DM — ranked pace
steps.detail: Last CNS primer. Treat it like ranked speed — not warmup pace. Queue within 10 min of finishing.

steps.title: Pre-ranked mental prep
steps.detail: Eyes closed: visualize one full round with your drill mechanic executed correctly. ONE cue. Three exhales.

steps.title: Queue
steps.detail: Green mode. Trust the work. Play positions and chess — not mechanics.

cautions: No ranked — protect training day integrity
Track everything in Tracker — 60 min without notes is 60 min half-wasted

steps.title: RAMP — all 4 phases
steps.detail: Quality over speed on all phases.

steps.title: Drill A — deep deliberate practice
steps.detail: Your primary drill. Maximum conscious attention. Pink mode at full. No rushing past the completion condition.

steps.title: DM block — Guardian + Ghost
steps.detail: First 7 min: Guardian DM (trains stop timing — one clean shot per full stop, punishes any movement). Switch to Ghost for 8 min (trains placement and reading). Same drill cue for both.

steps.title: Drill B — secondary
steps.detail: Your secondary drill. Stay adjacent to Drill A in category. Build on what you drilled, not something completely unrelated.

steps.title: VOD review — 5 min hard cap
steps.detail: One clip. One death. 5-Why method. Write root cause in Tracker. Stop at 5 min — do not spiral.

steps.title: Tracker + idle rest
steps.detail: Log the session. Then 5 min eyes closed, no phone — hippocampus consolidates today's motor sequences.

steps.title: Idle rest — mandatory
steps.detail: Eyes closed. No phone, no input. Your hippocampus is currently replaying today's motor sequences in reverse — this is how motor memory forms. Checking a screen disrupts this window.

steps.title: Mental visualization
steps.detail: Replay the correct versions of your drill from today — the two-step peek done right, the breath before swing, the clean stop-and-shoot. 15 min = up to 35% additional skill consolidation. Upper motor neurons activate identically to physical practice.

steps.title: Prioritize sleep
steps.detail: Motor memory consolidates during NREM2 sleep (sleep spindles). 8h after training = 20–30% better accuracy next session. No sleep = no consolidation. This is where the skill actually gets stored.

steps.title: Idle rest — mandatory
steps.detail: No stimulus. Eyes closed. Let the hippocampus replay today's drill sequences. Checking your phone breaks this consolidation window.

steps.title: Visualization
steps.detail: Imagine the drill you just trained — done perfectly. Slow, deliberate mental reps. Upper motor neurons activate identically to physical practice. This is free training.

steps.title: Sleep
steps.detail: Everything you drilled consolidates into permanent motor memory during NREM2. Protect sleep above all training decisions.
